metadata.tags: Headlines & Insights, Technologie, Media & Telecom, Retail, Economische ontwikkelingen, Sociaal ondernemen
metadata.author: Mario Bersem
metadata.description: Retail chains are developing their own media channels to sell advertising. This new media and advertising arm is growing at rocket speed. Read more.

[Technology, Media & Telecom](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/sectoren-en-trends/technologie/index.html). 01/11/2023 • 5 minutes read. [Mario Bersem](https://www.abnamro.nl/nl/zakelijk/insights/auteurs/mario-bersem/index.html). Retail chains are increasingly offering advertising space in their own channels: on the website, in the app, and also on screens in stores. Hema recently launched its own media network that connects all screens in its 280 stores. Hema is following supermarket chains like Albert Heijn and Plus, for whom retail media is already a lucrative side income. Where retailers have traditionally ranked among the biggest advertisers, they now also sell advertising. Retailers are increasingly offering suppliers ad space to advertise via their own channels. And of course retailers use the channels themselves too: “Hamsteren!”, as a well-known slogan from a supermarket chain goes. It is not new that retailers highlight some suppliers’ products for a fee; that has happened for years in analogue form, for example through special promotions or shelves in the store. What is new is the addition of their own digital channels and physical screens on the shop floor. Entire new media networks are emerging: so-called retail media. Using retail media to offer digital advertising is part of an international trend that, for once, did not start in the United States but in China. In China, e-commerce platforms like Alibaba and JD.com started selling digital ad space more than twenty years ago. Retail media has also penetrated further in the United States than in Europe. For example, retail giant Amazon already earns more from online advertising revenue than broadcasting giant YouTube. In 2022, it involved almost 38 billion dollars for Amazon, while YouTube stalled at about 30 billion dollars. For 2023, Amazon expects 20% growth for its advertising arm. Retail media spend up 42%. In the Netherlands too, advertising spend via retail media is skyrocketing. According to Rogier Lammers, chair of the retail media taskforce at industry association VIA, spend rose by 42% in 2022. Advertising spend thus reached around 210 million euros, equal to 6% of total digital advertising spend.

In 2022, it involved almost 38 billion dollars for Amazon, while YouTube stalled at about 30 billion dollars. For 2023, Amazon expects 20% growth for its advertising arm. Retail media spend up 42%. In the Netherlands too, advertising spend via retail media is skyrocketing. According to Rogier Lammers, chair of the retail media taskforce at industry association VIA, spend rose by 42% in 2022. Advertising spend thus reached around 210 million euros, equal to 6% of total digital advertising spend.

metadata.author: Mario Bersem

metadata.parent_text: The funnel starts broad at the top with ‘awareness’ and ends at the bottom with the purchase of a product or service. Lower funnel placements command relatively high prices because the chance of conversion (sale) is greater when you advertise close to the point of sale. Retailers can add further personalisation based on their customer data, which makes the ads even more effective and therefore more valuable. The cost side also looks favourable for retailers, since the infrastructure already exists in the form of website, app and store space. On top of that, they can use retail media channels for their own advertising messages.

Lower funnel placements command relatively high prices because the chance of conversion (sale) is greater when you advertise close to the point of sale. Retailers can add further personalisation based on their customer data, which makes the ads even more effective and therefore more valuable. The cost side also looks favourable for retailers, since the infrastructure already exists in the form of website, app and store space. On top of that, they can use retail media channels for their own advertising messages.

text: High revenue and low costs mean high margins. According to an [estimate by Boston Consulting Group](https://www.bcg.com/publications/2022/how-media-is-shaping-retail), online could generate a gross margin of 70-90%.
